behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: yes—mocked commit graphs and timestamps can miss real git ancestry/age bugs. `REAL-INFRA`: yes—needs real git repo state and typically git subprocess calls. `MOCK-HIDE`: yes—mocking PR lookup and branch divergence can falsely pass. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, make `main` and a feature branch with commits not reachable from `main`, backdate commit timestamps beyond threshold, ensure no PR exists for that branch in the PR provider, run the real staleness detector via its normal command path, and assert it reports `stale-no-pr=true` with correct branch/age context.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: yes—hook/bootstrap wiring failures are often missed in pure in-process tests. `REAL-INFRA`: yes—session bootstrap path, current branch resolution, and output channel behavior are OS/subprocess-coupled. `MOCK-HIDE`: yes—mocked startup entrypoints can pass while real startup does not emit warning.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the real session bootstrap entrypoint in a temp repo whose checked-out branch is stale-no-PR, capture startup output, and assert a warning is emitted containing actionable context (branch name, staleness reason, and next action such as opening PR/rebasing).

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` output distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)".
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: yes—CLI command routing plus live repo state can fail despite unit-tested formatter logic. `REAL-INFRA`: yes—command execution path and branch/repo inspection commonly use subprocess/filesystem. `MOCK-HIDE`: yes—mocking detector output can hide regressions in actual command integration.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke `/kaizen-wip` through the real command interface in two real repo setups: (1) clean branch state and (2) branch with unmerged old commits and no PR. Assert outputs are distinct and specifically include `STALE` only in case (2).

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: yes—PR-state interpretation bugs (open vs merged) can be masked by simplistic mocks. `REAL-INFRA`: yes—requires real PR metadata retrieval path (API/CLI) plus git branch state. `MOCK-HIDE`: yes—hardcoded mock PR responses often always satisfy expected shape and miss real-state edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real repo connected to a PR backend, create branches that have (a) open PR and (b) merged PR, run staleness detection through production path, and assert neither is classified as `stale-no-pr` even when commits are older than threshold.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: yes—hash rewrites and ancestry changes are exactly the kind of git-graph behavior mocks miss. `REAL-INFRA`: yes—needs real rebase/squash operations and reachability checks in an actual git repo. `MOCK-HIDE`: yes—simulated commit IDs can hide logic that incorrectly keys on old hashes.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp real git repo, create stale candidate branch, then validate two transitions: (1) rebase branch onto updated main and (2) squash-merge branch work into main (changing hashes). Re-run detector after each and assert branch/work is not reported as unPRed stale when work is already represented in main or tied to merged PR state.
